hotspots.title: Vox’s ‘national priority’ surge in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Vox’s ‘national priority’ policy, demanding Spaniards be prioritized for public services and aid, has become a dominant theme in Spanish regional politics. The policy is now central to coalition agreements with the PP in Extremadura, Aragón, and Valencia, sparking legal and moral debates over discrimination and constitutional compliance.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its polarizing nature, framing it as a moral and legal crisis that challenges Spain’s constitutional values on equality and non-discrimination.

hotspots.title: Pete Hegseth’s Pentagon purges and Iran tensions
hotspots.blurb: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s aggressive military leadership reshuffle continues, with the firing of Navy Secretary John Phelan and Army Chief of Staff Randy George amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. The Strait of Hormuz blockade and Iran’s naval provocations dominate coverage, with Hegseth framing the conflict as a test of American resolve.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its high-stakes geopolitical framing, military leadership instability, and the perceived existential threat to U.S. interests in the Middle East.

hotspots.title: Hungary’s Tisza Party landslide and reforms
hotspots.blurb: Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party secured a historic two-thirds majority in Hungary’s April elections, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. The new government promises sweeping reforms, including opening communist-era secret police archives, restructuring ministries, and adopting the euro, while facing immediate challenges from EU legal rulings and economic pressures.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its historic nature, the abrupt end of Orbán’s authoritarian rule, and the high-stakes reform agenda that could reshape Hungary’s political and economic landscape.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right AfD continues to surge in polls, particularly in eastern states like Saxony-Anhalt, where it leads ahead of autumn elections. The party’s radicalization, personnel scandals, and calls for a wealth tax dominate coverage, while mainstream parties scramble to contain its influence through parliamentary reforms and legal challenges.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the AfD’s growing electoral threat, its extremist rhetoric, and the broader democratic backlash it has provoked, including debates over party bans and constitutional safeguards.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s ‘patriotic’ school agenda
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK’s proposal to mandate Union Jack displays, King’s portraits, and a ‘patriotic’ history curriculum in schools has sparked intense debate. The party’s controversial candidates, including those with far-right ties, and its push to scrap ‘gold-plated’ public sector pensions further fuel the controversy.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its nationalist framing, the moral charge of imposing a ‘patriotic’ curriculum, and the party’s growing influence in local elections, which threatens to reshape British education and politics.
